votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq functions, using ord/chr for ASCII conversions and list comprehensions for the recursive mapping, preserving the exact logic of the Caesar cipher shift.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq logic, implementing the same character rotation and string manipulation functions.
The Python code correctly translates the Coq logic, using equivalent string and character manipulation functions.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. is_lowercase checks if ASCII code is in [97,122], rotate_char shifts lowercase letters by 4 positions with wraparound (mod 26), encrypt_aux applies rotate_char to each character, and encrypt_spec checks if result equals the encrypted string. All operations produce the same concrete results as the Coq code.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. is_lowercase checks if ASCII code is in [97,122], rotate_char shifts lowercase letters by 4 positions with wraparound mod 26, encrypt_aux applies rotate_char to each character, and encrypt_spec checks if result equals the encrypted string. All operations produce the same concrete results as the Coq code.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. is_lowercase checks if ASCII code is in [97,122], rotate_char shifts lowercase letters by 4 positions with wraparound mod 26, encrypt_aux applies rotate_char to each character, and encrypt_spec checks if result equals the encrypted string. All concrete computations match between Coq and Python.

Both implement a Caesar shift by 4 on lowercase ASCII letters only, leave other characters unchanged, and compare result strings via the same string/list conversions. For all ASCII inputs, behavior matches the Coq spec.

Both implement a Caesar shift by 4 on lowercase ASCII (a-z) with wraparound and leave all other characters unchanged; conversions between strings and lists match, and the spec checks equality of the transformed string.
Python mirrors the Coq spec: shifts lowercase ASCII by 4 with wraparound, leaves other chars unchanged, and the list/string conversions and equality check align.
